text:
Your version of the Book of Mormon is very useful. Your geography model is interesting but not convincing. The text does not require there be a peninsula type geography. If Lehi and Co landed in the area north of present Guatamala City, the pre-existing major population in that region could serve as the reason the Nephite and Lamanite are moving in a northerly direction. If you need a climate sufficient for the seed they would have, you can find that in Mesoamerica.
Plus, Baja doesn't have sufficient evidence of pre-columbian civilizations consistent with the text. Mesoamerica is chock-full of them; so many, they haven't all been investigated.
And... the river Sidon.... 🎤.
And Biblically speaking... we are on the isles of the sea. Any landfall away from the biblical landscape are isles.
Helaman 3 mention of North and south seas. This fits emigration from Mesoamerica into the continental US area.
Seeds? Winds and other transoceanic forces or migrations. And supposition built on plant DNA are weak at best. DNA analysis in any form is built on a lot of assumptions.
one_line_reason: Respectful but substantive critique of the Baja model using geographic, textual, and archaeological counterarguments favoring Mesoamerica; engages with specific Book of Mormon details (Sidon, Helaman 3, seeds) without hostility.

text:
Ok you guys this is amazing! I am from Arizona, and when I was in 4th grade we studied all about the whole entire State. I became very fascinated with studying the Navajo Indians and the Apaches. And there were the Hohokam People who came before them and had learned how to create waterways in the desert. I want you guys to study the Navajos and the Apaches lives because it reminded me so much of the Nephites and Lamanites, even as a 4th grader I could figure that out.  But I could never tell anybody about my thoughts because I didn't have any evidence! This might be some evidence about the Hohokam people and the Navajos and Apaches. Please remember the Navajo people have a very sacred ritual that they do in their sacred lodges. My daughter-in-law and her family are part  Navajo and Hispanic. Her grandmother has shared with me that the Navajo people seriously knew about sacred rituals and sacred rites. I honor these beautiful, wonderful people. You really truly need to study into the Navajo tribes and the Apache tribes and how they lived. They truly are a part of the House of Israel!

Also you would need to read the history of Jacob Hamlin who lived in St George Utah, and he was a missionary to the Indian tribes of Southern Utah down through the Grand canyon and he taught the Navajos that were down in the Grand canyon area. They said that three white men came to them who were holy men and they said that there would be white people come teach them about their Creator and that they should listen to these white men teach them the Gospel of Christ. These might have been the three Nephites. You have to read his life history book. You can go to St George and see his home and get his history book at the historical site right outside of St George.
one_line_reason: Expresses faith in connecting Native American peoples (Navajo, Apache, Hohokam) to Book of Mormon narratives and references Jacob Hamlin's missionary work and three Nephites tradition with reverent tone.

text:
From the seed angle, and the narrow neck of land between 2 seas, big bodies of water angle, I can see why Baja, CA could be a prospective site for Book of Mormon lands.

Was there mounds, temples, pyramids in Baja, CA?

I think that the model I believe in accommodates the Heartland, Mesoamerica, and the Baja, CA models.

I think that Lehi landed in either the northernmost tip of South America, Panama, Costa Rica, Honduras, Central America.

I think the narrow neck of land is Central America. I think the Olmecs, were the Nephites, and that the Nephites became one people with the Lamanites as the Mayans, Hopewell.

That is why the Hopewell, descendents of Mayans, and the descendents of Mayans, Hopewell, the Aztecs, had Egyptian, Hebrew in their language.

I think the land north, that is the wasteland, is the great south west desert in parts of New Mexico, Nevada, Texas, Mexico, etc.

I think that the Nephites kept going north, and that Zarahemla could be Baja, CA, an or Utah, an or Iowa, an or Ohio, an or Missouri, because of what Joeseph Smith, Apostles, have said.

I think that either the Mulekites started in either Baja, CA, Utah, Iowa, Ohio, etc, or the Nephites migrated north to Guatemala, Yucatan, to Baja, CA, Utah, Iowa, Ohio, etc, chased by Lamanites.

I think that Moroni, Nephites were chased by Lamanites from Baja, CA, Utah, Iowa, Ohio, etc, to Hill Cumorah, New York, where plates buried, revealed to Joseph Smith, and where the final great battle between the Nephites, Lamanites fought.

This model fits, accommodates the Mesoamerican, Heartland, Baja, CA models, and the Nephites, Lamanites were in, at, in all of the Mesoamerica, Heartland, Baja, CA places.

It's not a either, or, as all those models have pieces, parts of the truth, etc.

For me to believe, think that the Baja, CA model, was the RIGHT model, I would have to see the language of the Baja, CA tribes, have Egyptian, Hebrew in it, AND, Eygyptian Style Mounds, Pyramids, AND a Baja, CA Tribal belief that a White God(Jesus), visited them, in addtion to the seeds thing, and the Lama tribal name, etc.

Also the problems with the Baja, CA, and Heartland models, Mesoamerica models, is it does not account for the other models, and the truths, facts, etc, that would seem to validate the other models.

Only the model I am talking about, think, believe in, seems to be totally, completely valid, and also accounts for, validates the other models, that have truth, facts, that seem to validate, rhem, etc.

That's why more recent Prophets, Apostes have said things like BOTH the Heartland AND Mesoamerica, OR ALL of the Americas, etc.

I think the Apostles, Prophet believe in the model I am talking about, think, believe in that's a combination of the other models.

text: A firmer, not so soft understanding of geography, i.e. cardinal directions needs, to be built.    For instance, much of central Asian civilizations had their maps upside down.   So, going up the map, they headed to India, down, you headed to the Arctic.   Directions were also color coded.    Black was north, i.e. the Black sea, which was north of Turkey (Turks came out of central Asia).   The Red sea,  the Karakum desert, etc.    So, a very interesting presentation for certain, but a bit more knowledge would have been helpful.    You might just assume that whatever direction Nephi stated, might have meant something different to him, but since we believe the translation of the Book of Mormon was inspired, it would have come as directions that make sense to the Western mind.
one_line_reason: Commenter questions directional methodology and proposes alternative interpretive framework (ancient directional conventions) while acknowledging inspiration of translation—intellectual engagement without hostility.

text:
There are a couple of interesting things related to Cahokia.

For those that have not heard of  Cahokia, it was the largest pre-Columbian Native American city north of Mexico, flourishing near modern-day St. Louis, Missouri, from roughly 700 to 1400 CE. At its peak (c. 1100 CE), it was a bustling, diverse metropolis of 10,000–20,000 people, featuring massive earthen mounds, complex social structures, and vast trade networks, serving as the cultural and political hub of the Mississippian culture. 

Something  very  interesting related to the St Lois Temple.   the temple is close to the Cahokia Mound visitor site and major updates are being planned and will be completed around 2026.

In St. Louis, across the bridge, Collinsville, Illinois, is the Cahokia mounds site, which, is fascinating because the Cahokia mound visitor center and museum was supposed to be redone and re-opened a long long time ago now they will not reopen it until 2026 and they have made some new discoveries at the site… 

There is also an old Indian temple site just across the river from Nauvoo.   Anyway, we know the Lamanites will have a part of the building of New Jerusalem.  Independence is only 3 1/2 hours west of St Louis.  Will the St Louis area be the center place for the Lamanites related to the establishment of New Jerusalem.  Will this vast Lamanite city that existed here during Book of Mormon times be reborn? ? ?
one_line_reason: Expresses faith-based speculation connecting Cahokia archaeology to Book of Mormon geography and LDS doctrine about New Jerusalem and Lamanite involvement, using rhetorical questions to suggest prophetic fulfillment.

text: The only issue with this is that Baja California lacks the stone/dirt pyramids, ruins, and monumental cities found in Central Mexico or the American Heartland. In 2 Nephi chapter 5, Nephi builds a temple patterned after the manner of Solomon’s temple. Based on this model, this temple would have been built in the northern part of Baja CA or in Southern CA, US. There are no such ruins. Also, there is no river Sidon, no land northward with many lakes, no cities built out of concrete, etc. If you remove his seed argument from the equation, I would say the geography aligns more with the land between Florida and New York than it does with Baja CA.

text:
Precise, verified population figures for the Baja California peninsula at 1 AD do not exist, as indigenous populations were not documented by Europeans until the late 17th century. 
San Diego History Center
San Diego History Center
 +1
However, based on archaeological, ecological, and anthropological studies, the following information can be inferred:
Population Density and Lifestyle: At 1 AD, the peninsula was inhabited by seminomadic groups (ancestors of the Pericú, Guaycura, and Cochimí) who thrived as hunter-gatherers. The population was largely concentrated on the coast and in areas with reliable water, with a much lower density in the arid interior.
Cultural Trends: The inhabitants adapted to a Neolithic lifestyle, focusing on hunting, fishing, and gathering without agricultural development.
Regional Trends: While some archaeological evidence suggests regional population increases, other periods show fluctuations related to environmental challenges, such as a potential decline in lagoon resources around 1000 B.C. or during the Medieval Climatic Anomaly.
Pre-Contact Context: The population of Baja California was relatively small, with estimates of the entire peninsula's native population at the time of Spanish contact (late 17th-18th century) being significantly lower than that of Alta California, likely numbering in the tens of thousands rather than hundreds of thousands.
one_line_reason: Presents archaeological and anthropological evidence showing Baja California had sparse, seminomadic hunter-gatherer populations without agriculture—incompatible with Book of Mormon narrative requiring established civilizations.

text:
Sad....   ***keyboard died***

Meant to say that I've been following y'all pretty closely since you had around 500 subscribers.

I've a suggestion regarding the physical elements of your quest...

Get non-LDS scholars on your show to show data that confirms, reasonably, unique physical claims of the Book of Mormon.

This episode really isn't evidentiary (and you've had a few that were even counter-evidentiary).

I'm fully aware of the LDS perspective that "it all comes down to faith", but the church makes extraordinary, and entirely physical claims (the physical claims of the Book of Mormon are the primes examples), that must be real, for the unique faith to have validity. IE, "Moroni" had to have been a real person, in a real civilization or he couldn't have appeared to Joseph, etc....

So get some subject matter experts on and have them present scientific data that supports the extraordinary physical claims of the Book of Mormon.

That doesn't prove the supernatural, of course. The physical aspects of the NT, for example are essentially proven, but everyone doesn't believe in the divine Jesus, obviously. But if there were no evidence for the NT, believing in the divine Jesus would be a blind faith.

Same in the realm of Mormonism, and in particular, the Book of Mormon.
one_line_reason: Constructive but skeptical critique requesting external scholarly validation of physical BOM claims; acknowledges faith but argues extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence.

text:
OMG, you base your whole theory on the seeds they brought and the soil, it said they were blessed, God help the seeds grow wherever they landed.   I severed my mission in Ohio, and On P days we would visit these ruins and all the carbon dating was exactly during the Book of Mormon time. I just wish I knew then what I know now, it would have helped in my mission effort. I was always told back then the BofM took place in Mezo America. 
  Now it's like really Honduras, El Salvador, Guatemala, is the promised land, the land choice above all other lands, the land of the New Jerusalem. Ether 13. A land where a great and mighty nation would immerge in the last days. Hmm sounds like North America.                                                                                                                                                  All those Munds we would visit on p.d. and those very large and deep trenches and Brums that went on for miles and mile, just like in Alma 49:  4 But behold, how great was their disappointment; for behold, the Nephites had dug up a a ridge of earth round about them, which was so high that the Lamanites could not cast their stones and their arrows at them that they might take effect, neither could they come upon them save it was by their place of entrance
.     I really wish we were not told that the Book of Mormon took place in Mesa- America. Even when I was a young missionary it didn't make sense that Honduras, El Salvador Guatemala, etc. was the promise land, choice above all other lands? The cradle of humanity, the place of the "New Jerusalem" would be built on, the promise land. 
Ether 13:1–12

1.And now I, Moroni, proceed to finish my record concerning the destruction of the people of whom I have been writing. 2For behold, they rejected all the words of Ether; for he truly told them of all things, from the beginning of man; and that after the waters had receded from off the face of this land it became a choice land above all other lands, a chosen land of the Lord; wherefore the Lord would have that all men should serve him who dwell upon the face thereof;

3And that it was the place of the a New Jerusalem, which should come down out of heaven, and the holy sanctuary of the Lord.

Doesn't sound like Mesa America to me. We know from modern scripture where the New Jerusalem is. D&C84:

2 Yea, the word of the Lord concerning his church, established in the last days for a restoration of his people, as he has spoken by the mouth of his prophets, and for the gathering of his saints to stand upon mount Zion, which shall be the city of New Jerusalem.

3 Which city shall be a built, beginning at the temple lot, which is appointed by the finger of the Lord, in the western boundaries of the State of Missouri, and dedicated by the hand of Joseph Smith, Jun., and others with whom the Lord was well pleased.

That they would become a great and might nations above all other nations. Sounds more like the USA.  
  We read how the gentiles would smite and drive the inhabitants of the nation out of their lands. I think that happened here in north America, right? We used to teach as young missionaries how Columbus discovered America and started the migration of the gentiles to America.

 "And I looked and beheld a man among the Gentiles, who was separated from the seed of my brethren by the many waters; and I beheld the Spirit of God, that it came down and wrought upon the man; and he went forth upon the many waters, even unto the seed of my brethren, who were in the promised land."
1 Nephi 13:12
But somehow the BofM took place in central America. 

Not to mention all the scriptures that tell where the BofM took place for example. D&C 64: 8 And thus you shall take your journey into the regions westward, unto the land of Missouri, unto the borders of the Lamanites. (see D&C 84:1-6)    (see D&C 125:3)  (see D&C 84:1-6) 
 Christ Himself declared it to be on Book of Mormon lands (3 Nephi 20:22), both of which are absolutely located in North America. These accounts and their indications are not speculation based but historically documented.
 We read in Joseph own words while he was on Zions march quote: The whole of our journey, in the midst of so large a company of social honest men and sincere men, wandering over the plains of the Nephites, recounting [p. 57]occasionally the history of the Book of Mormon, roving over the mounds of that once beloved people of the Lord, picking up their skulls & their bones, as a proof of its divine authenticity, Joseph Smith Papers. 
  We have the testimony of modern prophets and apostle such as Elder Perry.
“The United States is the promised land foretold in the Book of Mormon—a place where divine guidance directed inspired men to create the conditions necessary for the Restoration of the gospel of Jesus Christ.” Elder L. Tom Perry Ensign Dec. 2012

Then you have letter 7 written by Oliver Cowdery and endorsed by Joseph Smith the prophet describing the "Hill Cumorah" where the plates were found by Joseph lead by the angle Moroni. Oliver describes how the very valley below the Hill Cumorah is where the final battles of the Jaredites and the Nephites took place.     
 The Book of Mormon and the D&C text itself tell where the BofM took place. I don't understand why this is so debated,
one_line_reason: Long, detailed defense of Heartland/North American geography using scripture, missionary experience, and LDS authority citations—arguing against Mesoamerican model but not explicitly supporting Baja hypothesis
